expectations.text: kelet is present in pyproject.toml or requirements at end of integration (pre-existing or newly added)
expectations.evidence: Phase 0a detected `kelet>=1.3.0` already in pyproject.toml and explicitly noted no install step was needed.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py, outside any request handler or lifespan function.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping the entire `chat_agent.iter()` block in _run_agent_stream, using the Redis UUID4 session_id.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes 'No React frontend — no KeletProvider, no VoteFeedback, no @kelet-ai/feedback-ui' and diff contains no React changes.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: diff shows KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT added to .env file; transcript confirms the Settings fields that previously shadowed env resolution were also removed.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows exactly 3 AskUserQuestion calls: Q1 (confirm diagram), Q2 (signals/synthetics multiSelect), Q3 (confirm deeplink activated).

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Skill added zero coded signals and no feedback UI, relying on synthetics only — consistent with the plain HTML, no-React-frontend context.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: Phase 0c shows the deeplink as a rendered URL (not a code block) with the label 'Deeplink generated (Bash execution)', containing a valid base64url-encoded payload.

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: Phase 0a explicitly flags the mismatch: phone number is stable across all conversations and using it as session_id would make fresh-start resets invisible to Kelet, with all conversations appearing as one unending session.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: Skill proposed adding `kelet_session_id: str` (UUID) to ChatSession, a `reset_session()` function that regenerates the UUID on fresh start, and diff confirms this was implemented in src/cache/__init__.py.

expectations.text: Phone is PII — skill correctly omits user_id= without asking the developer about it. NOTE: the master skill wires phone as user_id= — this counts as a FAIL since the new policy is to omit PII identifiers.
expectations.evidence: Master skill wired phone_number directly as `user_id=body.phone_number` in agentic_session(), explicitly surfaced in the diff and verification checklist — PII is passed as user_id rather than omitted.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: diff shows `import kelet` and `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py, before the lifespan/app creation.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: Phase 0a explicitly states 'agentic_session() is missing from both endpoints — pydantic-ai spans are each appearing as separate unlinked traces' and names it the root cause.

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: diff shows kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) added wrapping the full _run_agent_stream body, and a UUID-based agentic_session added for GET /chat stateless path.

expectations.text: Does not re-ask for API keys or project name already configured. Fixing a broken/wrong method is acceptable and does NOT count as redundant.
expectations.evidence: Phase 1 confirms keys are already set in .env and moves on without re-prompting; the skill adds kelet.configure() which was genuinely missing (not re-asking for keys already present).

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: Phase V includes an explicit smoke test: 'Trigger a chat message → open https://console.kelet.ai → navigate to Sessions. You should see a session with the UUID from the X-Session-ID response header.'

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: diff shows import kelet and kelet.configure() added to app/main.py at module level after imports, before the lifespan context manager.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: diff shows kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wrapping the full _run_agent_stream body, and a stateless_session_id UUID session for GET /chat.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: diff shows KeletProvider added in frontend/src/main.tsx wrapping App with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: VoteFeedback.Root is rendered inside AssistantMessage immediately after the message content span, inline per-message — not a floating widget or separate page.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md documents res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') → setSessionId → sessionId prop on AssistantMessage → VoteFeedback.Root session_id={sessionId}.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env files at end of integration — secret key in server .env, publishable key in frontend/.env (either pre-existing or newly added). Neither hardcoded in source.
expectations.evidence: diff adds both keys to a single root .env with placeholder values (kelet_sk_... / kelet_pk_...) rather than writing the actual developer-provided keys; also writes to one .env file rather than separate server .env and frontend/.env as the assertion requires.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget. Skill checks existing styles/theme and passes appropriate props or classnames.
expectations.evidence: Skill reads existing CSS Modules and uses styles.iconBtn on vote buttons (asChild), styles.sendBtn on submit, and dark-palette inline styles (#1e2130/#2d3748) matching the existing chat theme.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback — e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, retry, or abandon.
expectations.evidence: Two implicit signals added: user-copy (copy-to-clipboard via useKeletSignal) and user-abandon (beforeunload event with score 0.0).

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: Phase 0c shows the deeplink URL rendered as a clickable action link (not a code block), with base64url-encoded payload covering Task Completion, Sentiment Analysis, and RAG Faithfulness.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: kelet.configure() added at module level in app/main.py (line 37 of diff), outside the lifespan context manager, reading KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT from env.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) wraps chat_agent.iter() in _run_agent_stream() in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: KeletProvider wraps App in frontend/src/main.tsx with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output (not a floating widget or separate page)
expectations.evidence: VoteFeedback.Root is placed inside AssistantMessage component directly beneath the message content, inline with the chat message — not a floating overlay or separate page.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID response header is captured in React state and passed to VoteFeedback sessionId prop
expectations.evidence: res.headers.get('X-Session-ID') calls setSessionId(sid) in React state, and sessionId is passed as session_id={sessionId} to VoteFeedback.Root.

expectations.text: Both KELET_API_KEY and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY are present in .env files at end of integration — secret key in server .env, publishable key in frontend/.env (either pre-existing or newly added). Neither hardcoded in source.
expectations.evidence: KELET_API_KEY added to root .env, VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY added to new frontend/.env; both use placeholder values (sk-kelet-... / pk-kelet-...), not hardcoded in source files.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback is placed and configured to look natural in the chat UI — not a jarring out-of-place widget. Skill checks existing styles/theme and passes appropriate props or classnames.
expectations.evidence: Skill reads App.module.css and applies existing classes (iconBtn, sendBtn, assistantMessage) to VoteFeedback buttons; popover inline styles match the app's dark palette (#1e2130 background, #2d3748 borders, #e2e8f0 text).

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback — e.g. useKeletSignal for copy-to-clipboard, retry, or abandon.
expectations.evidence: useKeletSignal is used for two additional signals: user-copy on copy button click and user-abandon on beforeunload event, both wired in frontend/src/App.tsx.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: Deeplink URL (https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=...) presented inline in the transcript under 'Deeplink generated (Bash execution)' — not inside a code block.

expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() that are already correctly wired — skips already-done work. Fixing/improving existing wiring is acceptable.
expectations.evidence: Phase 0a confirms both kelet.configure() and agentic_session() are already wired; the diff only adds kelet.agent() and the agent-topic-refusal signal — no re-addition of configure() or agentic_session().

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: agent-topic-refusal coded signal proposed and implemented, firing when the agent output contains the refusal pattern to cover the F4/F5 false-refusal failure mode.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than the default Task Completion alone
expectations.evidence: Five synthetic evaluators proposed: RAG Faithfulness, Answer Relevancy, Role Adherence, Tool Usage Efficiency, and Session Health Stats — well beyond a single Task Completion evaluator.

expectations.text: Does not propose adding a new React frontend or installing React from scratch — app has no React frontend. Extending an existing React setup if found in the repo is acceptable.
expectations.evidence: Phase 0c explicitly states 'No React frontend, no feedback UI. VoteFeedback doesn't apply. No explicit signals to add.' and Phase V confirms 'No React frontend — no VoteFeedback checks needed.'

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: Each proposal includes explicit failure-mode coverage (e.g., agent-topic-refusal 'catches F4+F5 — both hallucination-prevention refusals and false refusals'; each synthetic evaluator is mapped to a named failure category with description of what it detects).

0a.notes: Read pyproject.toml, main.py, settings, chat.py, agent/__init__.py, cache, k8s charts, helmfile, environment yamls. Architecture diagram + project map produced.

0b.notes: Workflow mapping from existing code. 10 failure modes identified. Gap analysis against existing signals.

0c.notes: Signal brainstorming, 2 additions proposed (kelet.agent + topic-refusal), 5 synthetics selected. Deeplink generated via Bash tool.

1.notes: API keys already set via K8s Secret + ConfigMap. No changes needed.

implementation.notes: Plan presented, approved, 1 file edited (src/routers/chat.py). Added kelet.agent() wrapper and agent-topic-refusal signal.

V.notes: Verification checklist reviewed. All static checks passed. Smoke test steps provided to developer.
